varieties.name: MR Buster
varieties.traits: Medium maturity, high tillering, industry benchmark for 32 years, excellent midge resistance (MR), good grain size
varieties.yield_notes: Consistently high yields 3.5-5.5 t/ha, excellent standability
varieties.growing_notes: Bred by Neil Muller. Dominated Queensland sorghum for decades. Excellent adaptation to subtropical Queensland conditions, performs in both dryland and irrigated systems. Extremely high and consistent grain yields. Good heat tolerance. Still widely grown by conservative growers who trust proven performance.

varieties.name: Resolute
varieties.traits: Medium maturity, improved pre & post flowering stress tolerance, limited tiller production, excellent stability
varieties.yield_notes: Consistently high across locations 3.5-4.5 t/ha, outperformed MR Buster in trials
varieties.growing_notes: Bred to surpass MR Buster. Won 1st place at 2023 RASQ Toowoomba Show dryland category. Excellent subtropical Queensland adaptation, particularly in Central Highlands. Superior stress tolerance makes it ideal for variable rainfall patterns. Performs well in tight finishes. Strong all-around hybrid for Brisbane region.

varieties.name: Halifax
varieties.traits: Similar maturity to Resolute, improved stress tolerance, consistent performer across environments
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding 3.4-6.8 t/ha depending on conditions, ranked top 3 in most Queensland trial sites
varieties.growing_notes: Also surpasses MR Buster with improved stress tolerance. Topped multiple NVT trial locations including Billa Billa (3.17 t/ha), Condamine (1.8 t/ha), Rolleston (3.79 t/ha), and Pampas (6.8 t/ha). Excellent heat and humidity tolerance. Well-suited to Brisbane's subtropical summer conditions. Versatile across soil types.

varieties.name: MR Taurus
varieties.traits: Medium maturity, midge resistant, good agronomics
varieties.yield_notes: Consistent mid-range performer
varieties.growing_notes: Reliable Queensland hybrid for subtropical conditions. Good midge resistance important for coastal regions. Handles Brisbane humidity well.

varieties.name: MR Bazley
varieties.traits: Midge resistant, proven Queensland performer
varieties.yield_notes: Stable yields in subtropical environments
varieties.growing_notes: Adapted to Brisbane region conditions. Good heat tolerance for subtropical summers.

varieties.name: Sentinel IG
varieties.traits: Herbicide tolerant (Imidazolinone), good agronomics
varieties.yield_notes: Good yielding with weed management advantages
varieties.growing_notes: Imidazolinone tolerance allows better weed control in Queensland conditions. Suitable for Brisbane subtropical climate.

varieties.name: A66
varieties.traits: Early-medium maturity, fast dry-down, robust early vigor, superior drought lodging tolerance
varieties.yield_notes: Consistent 2.5-3.8 t/ha in trials, dependable producer
varieties.growing_notes: Fast dry-down trait excellent for Brisbane's humid conditions. Exceptional rooting depth provides drought tolerance. Favorable early vigor establishes well in subtropical Queensland. Good heat tolerance. Performs well across soil types common to Brisbane region.

varieties.name: A75
varieties.traits: Medium maturity, excellent all-around hybrid, consistent across environments
varieties.yield_notes: Outstanding yields 2.6-7.1 t/ha, topped many trial sites including Pampas (7.12 t/ha), Capella/Dysart (4.28 t/ha), Duaringa (4.27 t/ha)
varieties.growing_notes: Top performer in Queensland NVT trials. Consistent performance in both dry seasons and favorable conditions - ideal for Brisbane's variable subtropical weather. Proven stress tolerance. Excellent across Central Queensland sites, indicating strong subtropical adaptation.

varieties.name: A50
varieties.traits: Early maturing, fast dry-down, can be paired with A14 or A66 to spread harvest window
varieties.yield_notes: Solid yields for early hybrid
varieties.growing_notes: Early maturity allows escape from late-season stress. Fast dry-down advantageous in humid Brisbane conditions. Good option for early planting in subtropical climate.

varieties.name: A14
varieties.traits: Slower dry-down than A50, can be paired with A50 to spread harvest window
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable yields
varieties.growing_notes: Provides harvest window flexibility in subtropical Queensland. Good adaptation to Brisbane region.

varieties.name: A90
varieties.traits: Medium-long flowering period, maximizes available moisture and nutrition before grain fill
varieties.yield_notes: High yield potential, quick dry-down for harvest
varieties.growing_notes: Maximizes Brisbane's subtropical growing season. Quick dry-down after grain fill benefits humid conditions. Excellent grain quality maintained under stress.

varieties.name: HGS-114 (Opticote Plus)
varieties.traits: Good disease tolerance, tested across Queensland growing districts
varieties.yield_notes: 3.33 t/ha at Clermont trials, solid performance
varieties.growing_notes: Opticote Plus seed treatment provides early protection. Tested extensively in Queensland subtropical conditions. Good adaptation to Brisbane climate and soil types.

varieties.name: Cracka (Opticote Plus)
varieties.traits: Consistent performer, good solid results across multiple locations
varieties.yield_notes: 1.67 t/ha at Condamine, consistent across central and southwest Queensland
varieties.growing_notes: Particularly noted for grain quality consistency in variable conditions. Maintains grain size under stress - important for Brisbane's variable subtropical weather. Proven across multiple Queensland sites indicating strong regional adaptation.

varieties.name: HGS-747 (Opticote Plus)
varieties.traits: Queensland-tested, Opticote Plus treated seed
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable yields across testing sites
varieties.growing_notes: Tested in Queensland subtropical conditions. Suitable for Brisbane region growing.

varieties.name: Liberty (Opticote Plus)
varieties.traits: Queensland-adapted hybrid
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields in subtropical environments
varieties.growing_notes: Suitable for Brisbane subtropical conditions. Good subtropical heat tolerance.

varieties.name: Agitator / Brazen
varieties.traits: Available untreated, Fully Loaded, or organic options
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable production
varieties.growing_notes: Agitator Organic option available for organic producers in Brisbane region. Queensland-adapted genetics.

varieties.name: Tanami
varieties.traits: Medium maturity, performs well in higher stressed sites
varieties.yield_notes: 2.47 t/ha at Kilcummin trial, strong stress performance
varieties.growing_notes: Particularly noted for stress tolerance - valuable for Brisbane's variable subtropical conditions. New commercial release backed by encouraging NVT results. Good performer when conditions are challenging.

varieties.name: Gibson
varieties.traits: Medium maturity, good stress performance
varieties.yield_notes: 3.78 t/ha at Capella/Dysart trials, performs well in higher stress sites
varieties.growing_notes: Companion release to Tanami with similar stress tolerance characteristics. Proven in Queensland trials. Good adaptation to subtropical stress conditions.

varieties.name: Caprock
varieties.origin: USA introduction, grown in Australia since 1900s
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, early Australian cultivar, hardy
varieties.yield_notes: Lower than modern hybrids but reliable

varieties.name: Martin
varieties.origin: USA, grown in Australia pre-hybrid era
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, commonly used until 1960s in Australia
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate by modern standards
varieties.growing_notes: Further developed as hybridization evolved in Australia. Adapted to Queensland subtropical climate historically. Seed-saving capable heirloom.

varieties.name: Wheatland
varieties.traits: Early-maturing grain variety, open-pollinated, drought tolerant, 3-4 feet tall
varieties.yield_notes: Very high yielding for open-pollinated type, excellent for northern climates
varieties.growing_notes: Commonly used in early Australian sorghum cultivation. Early maturity suits Brisbane's growing season. Excellent drought tolerance for dryland production. Suitable for subtropical Queensland. Seed-saving heirloom.
varieties.availability: Heritage seed suppliers (Mary's Heirloom Seeds, Thresh Seed Co.), limited in Australia

varieties.name: Alpha
varieties.traits: Open-pollinated, early Australian variety
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate yields
varieties.growing_notes: Used in Queensland before hybrid dominance. Adapted through decades to subtropical conditions. Seed-saving capable.

varieties.name: E57
varieties.traits: Longer maturity, initially very popular in Queensland
varieties.yield_notes: Mixed results depending on stored moisture and follow-up rain
varieties.growing_notes: Historical significance as one of first hybrids in Queensland. Results varied with Brisbane's inconsistent moisture patterns. Eventually replaced by quicker-maturing hybrids.

varieties.name: DK 55, DK 38 (Dekalb)
varieties.traits: DK 55 longer maturity (initially popular), DK 38 quicker maturing
varieties.yield_notes: DK 55 variable, DK 38 more consistent due to faster maturity
varieties.growing_notes: DK 38 achieved consistent results in Central Highlands and Callide Dawson Queensland due to quicker maturity. Better suited to Brisbane's limited stored moisture. Eventually replaced by newer genetics.

varieties.name: Goldrush, Prize, Nugget, Pride
varieties.traits: Quicker maturing than E57/DK 55, addressed moisture limitations
varieties.yield_notes: More consistent than longer-season predecessors
varieties.growing_notes: Important transition era addressing Queensland's moisture constraints. Faster maturity suited Brisbane's subtropical conditions better. Paved way for modern genetics.

varieties.name: Dale
varieties.traits: 10-12 feet tall, dense stalks with chalky white centers, high sugar content, disease resistant (leaf anthracnose and stalk rot)
varieties.yield_notes: High syrup production per acre, 110-120 days to harvest

varieties.name: Sugar Drip
varieties.traits: Up to 12 feet tall, thick juicy stalks, amber-red seed heads when mature, early-maturing
varieties.yield_notes: Good syrup production, 105 days to harvest, drought tolerant

varieties.name: Black Amber (Texas Black Amber Molasses)
varieties.origin: China (Tsungming Island, Yangtze River mouth), introduced to USA 1854 from France; known locally as 'loo-tsoh'
varieties.traits: 8-9 feet tall, juicy stems, highly branched pendulant inflorescences, early-maturing, also known as 'Chinese sugarcane'
varieties.yield_notes: Good syrup production, 100 days to harvest, forage/silage use

varieties.name: Texas Seeded Ribbon Cane (Gooseneck)
varieties.origin: Southern USA (North Carolina, Mississippi 1870s), formerly known as Gooseneck
varieties.traits: 8-9 feet tall, juicy green stalks, highly branched inflorescences, striking appearance
varieties.yield_notes: Very good syrup yields, approximately 120 days to harvest

varieties.name: Mennonite Sweet Cane
varieties.traits: Tall, vigorous, reliable producer, Missouri heirloom
varieties.yield_notes: High syrup yields, reliable producer
varieties.growing_notes: Grown by Mennonite community for generations. Reliable, vigorous growth. Dual-purpose syrup and grain. Heat-loving, suitable for Brisbane warm season. Multi-use: syrup, grain for baking, pancakes.

varieties.name: Kansas Orange
varieties.traits: Traditional sweet sorghum, regional legacy variety
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable syrup producer

varieties.name: Sumac (Early Sumac)
varieties.traits: Early-maturing, historically important sweet sorghum
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields for early variety

varieties.name: Honey Drip, Umbrella, Della, Simon, M81E, Theis, Top 76-6, Keller
varieties.traits: Honey Drip 105 days; Umbrella 112 days; Della 114 days; Simon 100 days; M81E 135 days; various maturities and characteristics
varieties.yield_notes: Variable yields, M81E longest season highest potential
varieties.growing_notes: Multiple options for Brisbane growers with different maturity preferences. Simon (100 days) and Honey Drip (105 days) are early options. M81E (135 days) for full-season production. All heat-tolerant, suitable for subtropical Queensland summers.
varieties.availability: Mississippi Agricultural and Forestry Experiment Station (MAFES) certified seed for Dale, Theis, M81E, Top 76-6

varieties.name: Martin Milo
varieties.traits: Medium-early open-pollinated bronze grain sorghum, 3-4 feet tall, semi-compact head, excellent drought tolerance
varieties.yield_notes: Good for open-pollinated type, 120 days
varieties.growing_notes: Selected in 1937 from milo disease-infested field for outstanding resistance. Excellent drought tolerance for Brisbane dryland conditions. Wildlife uses. Shorter stature manageable in Brisbane home gardens. Seed-saving capable heirloom.

varieties.name: Hungarian Broom Corn
varieties.traits: 7-10 feet tall, fast growing, long stems for broom making, drought tolerant
varieties.yield_notes: Good grain production plus broom material

varieties.name: White Popping Sorghum
varieties.traits: Grain sorghum bred for popping like popcorn
varieties.yield_notes: Good grain production

varieties.name: CSH 9
varieties.origin: ICAR-Indian Institute of Millets Research, India
varieties.traits: Popular rainy season hybrid, 13,000+ tonnes seed sales annually, superior adaptability, resistance to leaf diseases
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding, consistent performance
varieties.growing_notes: Most popular rainy season hybrid in India with massive adoption. Excellent disease resistance critical for Kerala's high humidity and monsoon conditions. Performs well under heavy rainfall. Suitable for Kerala's tropical monsoon from June-September. Good grain mold tolerance.
varieties.availability: IIMR, state agricultural departments, widespread in Kerala seed dealers

varieties.name: CSH 14
varieties.origin: Bred on new parental lines AKMS 14A x R 150, India
varieties.traits: Rainy season hybrid, 8-10 days earlier than CSH 9, 33% higher grain yield than CSH 1, better adaptability
varieties.yield_notes: 33% yield advantage over CSH 1, high yielding
varieties.growing_notes: Rapidly replaced CSH 1 due to superior performance. Earliness advantage in Kerala monsoon allows harvest before peak disease pressure. Better adapted to tropical monsoon conditions. Excellent choice for Kerala Kharif season. Earlier maturity beneficial for Kerala's intense monsoon.
varieties.availability: State seed corporations, Kerala agricultural dealers

varieties.name: CSH 16
varieties.origin: Developed from new ms line 27A x R line C 43, India
varieties.traits: High yielding rainy season hybrid, improved grain mold tolerance, compact panicle, long loose panicle with bold round seed
varieties.yield_notes: Further yield improvement over CSH 14, recorded 4961 kg/ha in Zone II trials
varieties.growing_notes: Improved grain mold tolerance critical for Kerala's high humidity and heavy monsoon rainfall. Ethiopian germplasm (IS 23549) provides disease resistance genes. Compact panicle reduces moisture retention. Excellent for tropical monsoon adaptation. Bold grain preferred by consumers.
varieties.availability: State agricultural departments, Kerala seed dealers

varieties.name: CSH 18
varieties.traits: Rainy season hybrid, check variety in multiple trial zones
varieties.yield_notes: 3001-4357 kg/ha depending on zone, reliable performance

varieties.name: CSH 23
varieties.origin: Developed through MS 7A x RS 627; M 35-1 (local Maharashtra landrace) is one parent, India
varieties.traits: Grain sorghum hybrid utilizing indigenous landrace genetics
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding
varieties.growing_notes: Incorporates M 35-1 landrace genetics providing tropical adaptation. Indigenous genetics provide monsoon tolerance. Suitable for Kerala conditions.

varieties.name: CSH 25
varieties.origin: Developed through PMS 28A x C 43, India
varieties.traits: Grain sorghum hybrid, utilizes diverse genetic base including Ethiopian germplasm
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding with improved combining ability
varieties.growing_notes: New male sterile line provides yield boost. Tropical adaptation through diverse genetics. Suitable for Kerala monsoon cultivation.

varieties.name: CSV 15
varieties.origin: Developed from SPV 462 x CSV 13, released 1990s, India
varieties.traits: Dual-purpose variety (grain + fodder), gaining popularity, high adaptability, excellent grain mold resistance
varieties.yield_notes: 2658 kg/ha (base), 3820-4961 kg/ha in trials, very high fodder

varieties.name: CSV 13
varieties.origin: Multiple cross with exotic and local genetic base, India
varieties.traits: High grain yielding, medium height, parent to CSV 15
varieties.yield_notes: High grain production

varieties.name: SPV 462
varieties.origin: Coimbatore, developed from multiple cross (IS 2947, IS 3687 from USA; IS 1151, BP 53 locals from Maharashtra & Gujarat), India
varieties.traits: Rainy season variety, very popular, parent to CSV 15, excellent grain mold adaptation
varieties.yield_notes: Outstanding yields 6393-6483 kg/ha in Zone I, 2778-3308 kg/ha various zones

varieties.name: SPV 1786
varieties.traits: Rainy season variety, improved over CSV 15
varieties.yield_notes: 7% higher than CSV 15 check in Zone II trials
varieties.growing_notes: Significant improvement over already-excellent CSV 15. Adapted to monsoon conditions. Suitable for Kerala Kharif cultivation.

varieties.name: CSV 27
varieties.traits: Rainy season variety, dryland adapted
varieties.yield_notes: Top performer 31.6 q/ha in Jharkhand dryland trials
varieties.growing_notes: Excellent dryland performance translates well to Kerala's erratic rainfall patterns. Performs when water is limiting. Good monsoon adaptation.

varieties.name: CSV 18, CSV 20, CSV 35F
varieties.traits: CSV 18 (2005) very good grain quality from CR 4 x IS 18370, early maturing 90-95 days; CSV 20 (2006) uses SPV 946; CSV 35F forage variety
varieties.yield_notes: Good yields across applications
varieties.growing_notes: CSV 18's early maturity (90-95 days) beneficial for Kerala monsoon - allows harvest before peak disease pressure. CSV 20 recent release with improved genetics. CSV 35F provides forage option for Kerala dairy/livestock farmers. All adapted to tropical monsoon.

varieties.name: SPV 2579
varieties.traits: Top variety for grain yield, mold tolerance, developed from diverse genetic backgrounds including landraces
varieties.yield_notes: Outstanding 4069 kg/ha, 44.93 kg/ha/yr genetic gain over CSV 15
varieties.growing_notes: Represents pinnacle of modern sorghum breeding in India. Top performer for both grain yield AND mold tolerance - critical combination for Kerala monsoon. 53% yield increase over 1990s CSV 15 baseline. Incorporates landrace diversity for stress tolerance. Highest recommendation for Kerala commercial production. Latest genetics optimized for tropical monsoon.
varieties.availability: IIMR, state agricultural departments, newest release

varieties.name: SPV 2578, SPV 2678
varieties.traits: Top grain yield performers, modern breeding advances, stress tolerance
varieties.yield_notes: Among top 3 varieties for grain yield performance
varieties.growing_notes: Companion high-yielding releases to SPV 2579. Modern genetics optimized for tropical conditions. Excellent Kerala monsoon adaptation. Top choices for commercial grain production.

varieties.name: PYPS 2, PYPS 8
varieties.origin: India, developed from pool of 11 parental landraces, currently in evaluation
varieties.traits: Top performers for fodder, shoot fly tolerance, grain mold tolerance, high protein (PYPS 8 especially)
varieties.yield_notes: High grain and fodder yields, stable performance
varieties.growing_notes: Unique breeding from 11 landrace parents provides exceptional genetic diversity. Top choice for shoot fly tolerance (major Kerala pest). Outstanding mold tolerance for monsoon. PYPS 8 highest protein content - valuable for nutrition. PYPS 2 top fodder producer for Kerala livestock. Both combine yield, stress tolerance, and quality. Represent future of landrace-based breeding for tropical monsoon.
varieties.availability: IIMR, upcoming commercial release, evaluation stage

varieties.name: SPV 2769, SPV 2679
varieties.traits: Top fodder yield performers, high protein varieties
varieties.yield_notes: Among top 3 for fodder yield and protein content

varieties.name: SPV 2179
varieties.traits: Top shoot fly tolerance, important pest resistance
varieties.yield_notes: Stable yields with pest tolerance
varieties.growing_notes: Among top 3 for shoot fly tolerance - major pest in Kerala. Pest resistance reduces input costs. Reliable performance in Kerala's tropical conditions.

varieties.name: M 35-1 (Maldandi)
varieties.origin: Maharashtra landrace, developed over 75 years ago (1940s)
varieties.traits: INDIGENOUS variety, more senescent under stress, dominates post-rainy sorghum areas, tall 2.7m, 76-82 days flowering
varieties.yield_notes: 620 g/m² (6200 kg/ha), still occupies significant area despite age

varieties.name: CSV 216R (CSV 16)
varieties.traits: Marginal improvement over M 35-1, American germplasm lines utilized
varieties.yield_notes: Improved over M 35-1
varieties.growing_notes: Recent release attempting to improve on M 35-1 standard. Post-rainy adapted. For Kerala, would need Rabi irrigation. Combines landrace genetics with modern breeding.

varieties.name: SPV 2217
varieties.traits: Drought tolerant, maintains greenness at physiological maturity, stay-green trait, adaptable to water-limited conditions
varieties.yield_notes: 22.75 q/ha vs 19.10 q/ha for local, higher B:C ratio 2.42

varieties.name: CSH 19R
varieties.origin: Developed through MS 104A x AKR 354 (uses M 35-1 genetics through Swati parent), India
varieties.traits: Rabi (post-rainy) grain sorghum hybrid, incorporates M 35-1 landrace genetics
varieties.yield_notes: High yielding for post-rainy season
varieties.growing_notes: Post-rainy hybrid utilizing legendary M 35-1 genetics. For Kerala: Rabi season (Oct-Feb) with supplemental irrigation. Capitalizes on residual monsoon moisture plus irrigation. Hybrid vigor with traditional adaptation.

varieties.name: CSV 22R
varieties.traits: Higher root biomass for drought tolerance
varieties.yield_notes: Stable under water stress
varieties.growing_notes: Superior root system for post-monsoon drought. For Kerala Rabi with minimal irrigation. Good option for water-limited situations.

varieties.name: CSV 19SS
varieties.origin: Developed using RSSV 2 x SPV 462, released 2004, India
varieties.traits: Sweet sorghum variety for syrup/jaggery/ethanol production, high sugar content in stalks
varieties.yield_notes: Good biomass and juice production

varieties.name: CSH 22SS
varieties.origin: Developed from ICSA 38 x SSV 84 (Ethiopian origin IS 23568), India
varieties.traits: Sweet sorghum hybrid, highest grain yield among sweet types tested
varieties.yield_notes: Highest grain yield in sweet sorghum evaluations
varieties.growing_notes: Best grain performer among sweet sorghums. Ethiopian germplasm provides tropical adaptation. Dual purpose: syrup production + significant grain harvest. Excellent for Kerala where both products valuable. Combines income streams.

varieties.name: SSV 84, SSV 74
varieties.traits: Sweet sorghum varieties for stalk juice, sugar traits, rabi (winter) season adaptation tested
varieties.yield_notes: Good ethanol yields, on par performance for biomass and sugar traits

varieties.name: CSH 20MF
varieties.origin: Developed through 2219A (Kharif Shallu selection) x UPMC 503 (Pant Chari 6 = IS 5977 India origin), India
varieties.traits: Multi-cut forage hybrid, excellent regrowth, cytoplasmic male sterility based
varieties.yield_notes: Superior multi-cut yields
varieties.growing_notes: Outstanding forage hybrid for Kerala dairy/livestock farmers. Multi-cut capability provides continuous fodder through growing season. Excellent for Kerala's dairy industry (significant in region). Monsoon-adapted forage production. Can be cut multiple times for green chop or silage. Utilizes indigenous Indian germplasm (IS 5977) for tropical adaptation.
varieties.availability: IIMR, state agricultural departments, Kerala forage dealers

varieties.name: CSH 24MF
varieties.origin: Developed through ICSA 467 x Pant Chari 6 (IS 5977), India
varieties.traits: Multi-cut forage sorghum hybrid, landmark development using ICRISAT breeding material
varieties.yield_notes: High biomass, multiple cuts per season
varieties.growing_notes: Landmark hybrid combining ICRISAT and indigenous Indian genetics. Multi-cut system ideal for Kerala's continuous dairy operations. Pant Chari 6 parent provides proven tropical adaptation (IS 5977 from India). Excellent green chop or silage quality. Perfect for Kerala's year-round livestock feeding needs.

varieties.name: Pant Chari 6
varieties.origin: Selection from IS 5977 (originated from India), India
varieties.traits: INDIGENOUS forage variety, important parent line for CSH 20MF and CSH 24MF, multi-cut capability
varieties.yield_notes: Good biomass production

varieties.name: CSV 35F, CSH 40F
varieties.traits: Forage sorghum variety (CSV 35F) and hybrid (CSH 40F), single cut and multicut types
varieties.yield_notes: Reliable forage yields

varieties.name: Karad Local
varieties.traits: Traditional Maharashtra landrace, parent to 296B line used in multiple hybrids (CSH 5, CSH 25, others)
varieties.yield_notes: Moderate by modern standards
varieties.growing_notes: 🌏 INDIGENOUS Maharashtra landrace. Foundation genetics for Indian hybrid program. Used in 296B parent line (Karad local x IS 3922 kafir durra). Critical importance in modern breeding. Traditional cultivation methods. Could potentially be grown in Kerala with traditional farming approach. Seed-saving landrace. Cultural and genetic heritage importance.
varieties.availability: Limited, farmers' seed systems, germplasm collections, NBPGR

varieties.name: PJ 8K
varieties.traits: Local Maharashtra variety used in early hybrid CSH 1 development
varieties.yield_notes: Traditional yields

varieties.name: BP 53
varieties.traits: Gujarat local used in SPV 462 development (parent to CSV 15)
varieties.yield_notes: Traditional performance

varieties.name: Swati (SPV 86 x M 35-1)
varieties.origin: India, developed from SPV 86 x M 35-1 Maldandi landrace
varieties.traits: Variety incorporating INDIGENOUS M 35-1 genetics, used in hybrid development (CSH 19R)
varieties.yield_notes: Improved over M 35-1

varieties.name: Black Amber (Chinese Origin)
varieties.origin: Originally from China (Tsungming Island, 1854), introduced via France/USA
varieties.traits: 8-9 ft tall, early-maturing 100 days, juicy stems, originally grown in subtropical Chinese conditions
varieties.yield_notes: Good syrup, multi-purpose (forage, syrup, ornamental)

varieties.name: Dale, Texas Seeded Ribbon Cane, Sugar Drip, Mennonite
Grain Production (Heirloom): Wheatland - early maturing, drought tolerant, proven Queensland history
Grain (Hybrid): CSH 9, CSH 14, CSH 16 - top hybrid performance, disease resistance, monsoon adapted
